# Pangaea (pangaea.blog)

> A hub for the spoken and the written word by Adam Pang: essays, a podcast, and an audio-first publication, all on one owned URL.

Pangaea is Adam Pang's personal publication, in the lineage of sive.rs, paulgraham.com, and Tim Ferriss's 5 Bullet Friday: an owned URL, plain durable text, multi-modal worldbuilding. Each post is a written spine with song, image, video, quote, or tool embeds when they genuinely belong. The homepage and Season 1 are the Pangaea Pilot, an audio-first monastic publication (serif, white on black, one terracotta accent).

The writing lane lives at /posts (searchable, wiki-linked, with a force-directed graph view). The spoken side runs through the Pilot podcast and /podcast. It is built with Astro (hybrid: mostly static, SSR for a password-gated /write editor where Claude scaffolds drafts that commit straight to the repo), deployed on Vercel. The git repo is the CMS.

## Key links
- [Home](https://pangaea.blog/): the Pangaea Pilot wordmark, about, and project list.
- [Pilot](https://pangaea.blog/pilot/): the audio-first Season 1 album page: tracklist, liner notes, pull quote.
- [Posts](https://pangaea.blog/posts/): the essays index with client-side search, sort, and tag filtering.
- [Graph](https://pangaea.blog/graph/): a force-directed network of all posts and their wiki-links.
- [Podcast](https://pangaea.blog/podcast/): the spoken lane, YouTube-hosted episodes.
- [About](https://pangaea.blog/about/): who is behind Pangaea and why.

## About
- Part of adam.inc, the internet portfolio of Adam Pangelinan (Anchor Marianas LLC).
- Builder: https://adampang.com
